llm_advice.rationale: Baseline ATen reference shows bottleneck is multiple GEMM and add launches. Fusing the rank-16 path (B.T @ X then A @ result) into a single custom kernel reduces launch overhead and improves memory locality, while keeping the large W @ X in cuBLAS avoids regressing the dominant GEMM. First candidate uses block size 256 and vector width 4 for good occupancy and bandwidth; subsequent candidates explore alternative block sizes and warp-level reductions to further optimize.

notes: Use ATen/cuBLAS for the d x d GEMM.
Explore kernels that fuse Y += A @ T for rank 16.
